onlineResources.note: Records in this series have been digitized and made available online by our partner, Footnote.com, for a fee. The digitized records on Footnote.com are available free of charge in all NARA Research Rooms, including those in our regional archives and Presidential Libraries.

title: Project Blue Book Administrative Files

referenceUnits.name: National Archives at College Park - Textual Reference

physicalOccurrences.physicalOccurrenceNote: Photographs are interfiled among the paper records in this series.

microformPublications.note: This series can be found on microfilm roll numbers 85-87 of T1206.
microformPublications.title: T1206 - Project 'Bluebook.', 1947-1969.

ancestors.title: Records of Headquarters U.S. Air Force (Air Staff)

scopeAndContentNote: This series consists of the administrative files of Project Blue Book, the U.S. Air Force investigation into unidentified flying objects (UFOs) that took place between 1947 and 1969.  The files contain correspondence, staff studies, status reports, congressional hearing reports, press releases, transcripts of public and Air Force briefings, newspaper articles, and photographs.  The files relate to the organization and staffing of the project, the release to the public of information about unidentified flying objects, and the progress and findings of the study.
functionAndUse: Air Force staff compiled these records as part of the Air Force study of UFOs called Project Blue Book.   Investigators from districts and commands sent the records to the Air Technical Intelligence Center at Wright Patterson Air Force Base.
